title: Holosepalum humifusum
taxonomy: Holosepalum humifusum is classified as a member of the family Commelinaceae, commonly known as the spiderwort family. It is part of the genus Holosepalum, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical regions. The Commelinaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that include species with a wide range of growth habits. The exact classification of Holosepalum humifusum is at the level of family and genus, as it is a specific species within the Commelinaceae family. Further classification of this species would require a more detailed examination of its characteristics and relationships to other species within the family.

It is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. intro: Holosepalum humifusum, also known as the creeping Holosepalum or creeping Spilanthes, is a perennial herb belonging to the family Acanthaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Holosepalum humifusum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Holosepalum humifusum is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to alleviate digestive issues and respiratory problems.
